description: Letter from Daniel b. ʿAzarya (in the handwriting of his scribe), probably in Jerusalem, to ʿEli b. ʿAmram, in Fustat. The sender mentions the praises that he has heard about ʿEli b. ʿAmram from Moshe, a man who recently arrived in Jerusalem from Egypt. Daniel is not named in the letter or address, but can be identified by his ʿalāma, ישועה, which occurs at the end of the letter and next to the direction of the letter on verso. On verso there is the address of the letter. In a different hand, there is also a long text consisting of a collection of passages from the Babylonian Talmud, Ḥagiga 12; Moʿed Qaṭan 5, 9, 16, for liturgical-homiletic purposes. (Information from CUDL and Gil, Palestine, vol. 2 p. 693-694, #377). VMR
description_he: מכתב מדניאל בן עזריה (בכתב סופרו), כנראה מירושלים, לעלי בן עמרם, פוסטאט. השולח מזכיר את שבחי עלי בן עמרם ששמע ממשה שהגיע מירושלים ממצרים. דניאל מזוהה לפי עלמתו 'ישועה'. על הגב: כתובת המכתב ואוסף קטעים מהתלמוד הבבלי, חגיגה יב; מועד קטן ה, ט, טז.
